compatibilityLabel: React DOM 19.2.6 DOM text-content, HostText boundary, and dangerouslySetInnerHTML exclusion oracle

generation.method: checked runtime inventory plus exact npm tarballs extracted into a temporary node_modules tree
generation.probeIsolation: one Node child process per mode, render scenario, and probe kind; shouldSetTextContent evaluated from the extracted React DOM development bundle

localFastReactStatus.reason: Fast React has no public DOM root render path or wired HostText create/update/reset behavior for these oracle scenarios.

probeModes.reason: Development mode records React DOM text-content output, mutation boundaries, root errors, and diagnostics.
Production mode records the same text-content output and mutation boundaries without development-only diagnostics.

shouldSetTextContentScenarios.description: Primitive string children select the element text-content path.
The empty string still selects the element text-content path.
Number children select the element text-content path.
BigInt children select the element text-content path.
React DOM's internal predicate treats textarea as text-content even though Fast React keeps controlled form behavior blocked.
React DOM's internal predicate treats noscript as text-content while Fast React keeps noscript behavior outside the local claim.
Null and undefined children do not select text-content.
Boolean children do not select text-content.
Arrays of primitive children stay on the managed child reconciliation path.
React element children do not select text-content.
Symbol children do not select text-content.
Function children do not select text-content.
A dangerouslySetInnerHTML object with non-null __html selects a leaf shortcut.
An empty-string __html value is non-null and still selects the leaf shortcut.
Nullish __html does not select text-content or the raw HTML leaf shortcut.
A non-object dangerouslySetInnerHTML prop is not accepted by the predicate.
The predicate sees non-null __html as a leaf shortcut; the prop layer reports the children conflict separately.

renderScenarios.description: Primitive text children use the element text-content shortcut and update through the text setter boundary.
Switching between element text-content and managed children records the reset and host child boundaries.
Array text around an element stays on the HostText path and updates text nodes separately from element text-content.
Adding text before and after an existing element records HostText insertion boundaries.
SVG text-content and foreignObject HTML descendants keep namespace creation separate from text writes.
An SVG container supplies SVG child context when the rendered root is a text element.
dangerouslySetInnerHTML is a leaf shortcut that excludes HostText creation, then can switch back to managed text.
Nullish __html values do not install raw HTML and do not count as a text-content shortcut.
Providing children with non-null dangerouslySetInnerHTML reports the React DOM conflict and leaves no managed text child claim.
A malformed dangerouslySetInnerHTML object is rejected before any managed text child behavior is claimed.

fakeDomSubstrate.purpose: exercise React DOM text-content and HostText mutation calls deterministically in Node without adding a DOM dependency to the conformance workspace
fakeDomSubstrate.browserAttributeNameNormalizationMode: HTML namespace setAttribute/removeAttribute names are lowercased by the fake DOM before final tree snapshots
fakeDomSubstrate.innerHTMLParsingMode: innerHTML assignment is recorded as an assignment and is not parsed into child nodes
doesNotModel: native HTML parsing and browser outerHTML serialization
layout, CSS cascade, focus, selection, and accessibility tree behavior
custom element lifecycle callbacks and browser upgrade timing
controlled form wrappers, textarea value tracking, selection restoration, and noscript browser behavior

intentionalGaps.reason: This oracle records pinned React DOM behavior only; Fast React DOM text mutation behavior is not implemented or compared in this worker.

Client probes execute real React DOM 19.2.6 mutation code, but the DOM target is a deterministic fake DOM. The oracle records React DOM calls and final fake DOM state, not browser parser, layout, CSSOM, focus, or custom-element reactions.

React DOM's internal shouldSetTextContent predicate includes textarea and noscript, but Fast React keeps controlled forms, textarea value tracking, and noscript browser/server behavior excluded until dedicated implementation work exists.
dangerouslySetInnerHTML is recorded only as a leaf shortcut and exclusion from managed text children. This artifact does not implement or claim raw HTML parsing or browser DOM compatibility.
